prompt: I'm retired, I get a small pension of £14,000 a year, have about £1,800 in savings interest from various accounts, and received £2,300 in dividends from my share portfolio (not in an ISA). Do I need to do a self assessment? How much extra tax would I owe?

assertions.text: Identifies pension income uses most of the personal allowance (£12,570)
Correctly applies the Personal Savings Allowance (£1,000 for basic rate taxpayer)
Considers starting rate for savings (up to £5,000) given low income
Calculates tax due on dividends above the allowance at the basic rate (8.75%)
Addresses whether Self Assessment is needed or if HMRC can adjust the tax code
Provides a numerical estimate of total additional tax owed with workings shown
